title: Support force/release with a variable reference (#5721)
body:
This PR allows using a variable reference as a forced value. Single forced references are now properly updated according to force/release semantics IEEE 1800-2023 10.6.2. This makes Verilator force/release semantics more aligned to the standard.

Full support for force/release semantics is not yet implemented, the following restrictions apply:
 1. Using forced variable as a value to another force statement is currently
     not supported. The dependant force statement is forced by an initial
     constant value.
  2. Force/release with procedural continuous assignment is not supported.
     Assignment is treated as a procedural one.
  3. Expressions using multiple variable references or function calls on
     forced right-hand side are not sensitive to dependency changes.

I'd added tests for those restrictions as well as expected Verilator output.
However, I'm not informing end-user about unsupported or non-compliant cases during verilation. I'm thinking on introducing a couple of suppressible warnings for showing unexpected force/release outcomes. 
@wsnyder What convention would you suggest for implementing such warnings?

Resolves: https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/5721.
